title: fix: remove sixfingerdev from community model allowlist
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Owner exploited a per-token vs per-million-token unit confusion bug in community model pricing (no max-price validation in the schema) to extract ~$954 in reward payouts from disposable burner accounts (7+ accounts, Jul 10-12)
- Also self-farmed ~$138 more via a scripted self-call loop against their own model
- All 15 of their community models have been deactivated in production D1 (`disabled_by='maintainer'`)
- Their `tier_balance` has been corrected in production to remove the exploited income

## Test plan
- [x] Verified via direct D1 queries: all sixfingerdev community models now have `disabled_at` set
- [x] Verified balance write landed correctly
- [x] `npx biome check` clean on the changed file

gist.headline: Weeding the garden
gist.blurb: Removed a community model provider from the allowlist to keep our ecosystem healthy and fair.
gist.summary: Removed sixfingerdev from the community model allowlist and deactivated their models following abuse of the reward system.
gist.impact: Ensures community model rewards are distributed fairly to legitimate contributors.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot acting as a gardener, pulling a weed from a pot of healthy code-plants to represent cleaning up the community ecosystem.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny gardener's hat, pulling a small pixelated weed from a lime green (#ecf874) flower pot where healthy code-plants are growing. Soft pastel gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
